name: Configuration System
description: YAML + Pydantic v2 frozen-model configuration loading with fail-fast validation. Defines the complete configuration hierarchy: ExemplarConfig (top-level), CircuitConfig (stage ordering, parallelism, thresholds), ReviewerConfig (per-reviewer enablement, trust weight, scope patterns), LedgerConfig (field classification rules for secrets/PII/internal APIs via LedgerFieldRule), and ApprenticeConfig (shadow/canary/primary mode, storage path). Provides a default_config() factory that returns a fully valid out-of-the-box configuration. Supports cascading path resolution: explicit flag > EXEMPLAR_CONFIG env var > .exemplar/config.yaml in CWD > built-in defaults. Deep-merges partial YAML onto defaults and validates via Pydantic. All errors wrapped in a ConfigError hierarchy with clear messages. No Chronicler dependency to avoid circular imports; callers handle event emission.

types.name: ConfigSource
types.description: StrEnum indicating how the configuration file path was resolved. Used to report provenance of the active configuration.

types.name: ConfigError

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error description including resolved path context

validators.error_message: Error message must not be empty

fields.name: path
fields.description: The resolved config file path that caused the error, or None if no path was resolved

types.description: Base configuration error. All config-related exceptions inherit from this. Carries a human-readable message and optional resolved path for diagnostics.

types.name: ConfigFileNotFoundError

fields.name: message
fields.description: Error message including the missing file path

validators.error_message: Error message must not be empty

fields.name: path
fields.description: The filesystem path that was not found

types.description: Raised when an explicitly specified or env-var-resolved config path does not exist on the filesystem. Subclass of ConfigError.

types.name: ConfigValidationError

fields.name: message
fields.description: Summary of validation failures

validators.error_message: Error message must not be empty

fields.name: path
fields.description: The config file path that produced invalid content

fields.name: validation_errors
fields.description: List of Pydantic validation error dicts, each with loc, msg, and type fields

types.description: Raised when YAML content is syntactically valid but fails Pydantic model validation (type errors, constraint violations, cross-field invariant failures). Wraps the underlying Pydantic ValidationError details. Subclass of ConfigError.

types.name: ConfigParseError

fields.name: message
fields.description: YAML parse error details including line/column if available

validators.error_message: Error message must not be empty

fields.name: path
fields.description: The config file path that could not be parsed

types.description: Raised when the YAML file cannot be parsed (syntax errors, encoding issues). Subclass of ConfigError.

types.name: Path
types.description: pathlib.Path representing a filesystem path

types.name: OptionalPath
types.description: Optional filesystem path, None when no path is applicable

types.name: LedgerFieldRule

fields.name: pattern
fields.description: Regex pattern to match against diff hunk content.

fields.name: label
fields.description: Classification label to apply when pattern matches.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Human-readable description of what this rule detects.

types.description: A single Ledger classification rule: a regex pattern mapped to a classification label.

types.name: LedgerConfig

fields.name: rules
fields.description: Ordered list of classification rules. First match wins per label category.

fields.name: default_label
fields.description: Default label when no rule matches.

types.description: Configuration for Ledger field classification: rules for detecting secrets, PII, and internal APIs.

types.name: LedgerFieldRuleList
types.description: Ordered list of LedgerFieldRule instances

types.name: ReviewerConfig

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: Whether this reviewer is active.

fields.name: stage
fields.description: The stage this reviewer operates in.

fields.name: default_trust_weight
fields.description: Initial trust weight for Arbiter scoring.

validators.error_message: Trust weight must be between 0.0 and 1.0

fields.name: allowed_file_patterns
fields.description: Glob patterns for files this reviewer may access.

fields.name: denied_file_patterns
fields.description: Glob patterns for files this reviewer must not access.

fields.name: allowed_classifications
fields.description: Classification labels this reviewer may see.

fields.name: max_severity
fields.description: Maximum severity this reviewer may emit.

types.description: Per-reviewer configuration: enablement, default trust, and policy scope.

types.name: ReviewerConfigMap

fields.name: security
fields.description: Configuration for the security reviewer (OWASP, secret detection)

fields.name: correctness
fields.description: Configuration for the correctness reviewer (logic errors, null checks)

fields.name: style
fields.description: Configuration for the style reviewer (convention violations)

fields.name: architecture
fields.description: Configuration for the architecture reviewer (coupling, layer violations)

types.description: Map of reviewer name to ReviewerConfig. Keys are canonical reviewer identifiers.

types.name: CircuitConfig

fields.name: stages
fields.description: Ordered list of review stages to execute.

fields.name: parallel_stages
fields.description: Groups of stages that can run in parallel. Each inner list runs concurrently.

fields.name: stage_timeout_ms
fields.description: Default timeout per stage in milliseconds.

fields.name: block_threshold
fields.description: Minimum number of critical/high findings to trigger a block decision.

fields.name: warn_threshold
fields.description: Minimum number of medium findings to trigger a warn decision.

types.description: Configuration for Baton circuit routing: stage ordering, parallelism, and timeouts.

types.name: ApprenticeConfig

fields.name: phase
fields.description: Current learning phase.

fields.name: storage_path
fields.description: Directory path for learning state files.

fields.name: shadow_to_canary_threshold
fields.description: Accuracy threshold to progress from shadow to canary.

fields.name: canary_to_primary_threshold
fields.description: Accuracy threshold to progress from canary to primary.

types.description: Configuration for the Apprentice learning module: phase, thresholds, and storage.

types.name: ExemplarConfig

fields.name: circuit
fields.description: Circuit routing configuration.

fields.name: reviewers
fields.description: Per-reviewer configuration keyed by reviewer_id.

fields.name: ledger
fields.description: Field classification rules.

fields.name: apprentice
fields.description: Learning module configuration.

fields.name: chronicle_log_path
fields.description: File path for Chronicler JSON-lines log.

fields.name: kindex_store_path

fields.name: stigmergy_store_path
fields.description: File path for Stigmergy pattern store.

fields.name: seal_chain_path

types.description: Top-level configuration for the Exemplar code review service.

types.name: StringList
types.description: List of strings, used for stage names, patterns, etc.

types.name: ResolvedConfigPath

fields.name: path
fields.description: Resolved filesystem path, or None if using built-in defaults

fields.name: source
fields.description: How the path was resolved

types.description: Result of config path resolution: the resolved path (or None for builtin) and the source that provided it.

types.name: ConfigProvider

fields.name: load_config
fields.description: Protocol method signature: (path: Path | None = None) -> ExemplarConfig

types.description: Protocol (runtime_checkable) defining the config loading interface. Implementations must provide load_config(). Used for dependency injection and testability.

types.name: Severity
types.description: Severity level of a review finding, ordered from most to least critical.

types.name: ReviewStage
types.description: Named stage in the review circuit pipeline.

types.name: LearnerPhase
types.description: Apprentice learning phase progression stage.

types.name: ClassificationLabel
types.description: Ledger data classification label for diff content.

functions.name: default_config
functions.description: Returns a complete, valid ExemplarConfig with all default values. All four reviewers enabled with trust_weight=1.0, standard stage ordering (security, correctness, style, architecture), common secret/PII detection patterns in Ledger, and shadow-mode Apprentice. This is the canonical baseline onto which partial YAML overrides are deep-merged.
postconditions: Returned config passes all ExemplarConfig validators
All four reviewers (security, correctness, style, architecture) are enabled
CircuitConfig.stages contains at least: security, correctness, style, architecture
CircuitConfig.parallel_stages is a subset of CircuitConfig.stages
CircuitConfig.block_threshold >= CircuitConfig.warn_threshold
LedgerConfig contains at least one SECRET and one PII detection rule
ApprenticeConfig.phase is 'shadow'
All LedgerFieldRule patterns compile as valid regex

functions.name: resolve_config_path
functions.description: Resolves the configuration file path using a cascading resolution chain: (1) explicit path parameter if provided, (2) EXEMPLAR_CONFIG environment variable if set, (3) .exemplar/config.yaml in the current working directory if it exists, (4) None indicating built-in defaults should be used. Returns both the resolved path and the source that provided it. Does NOT validate that the file exists for explicit/env_var sources — that is load_config's responsibility.

inputs.name: explicit
inputs.description: Explicitly provided config file path, typically from CLI --config flag

postconditions: If explicit is not None, returns (explicit, ConfigSource.explicit)
If explicit is None and EXEMPLAR_CONFIG env var is set and non-empty, returns (Path(env_var_value), ConfigSource.env_var)
If no explicit or env var, and .exemplar/config.yaml exists in CWD, returns (cwd/.exemplar/config.yaml, ConfigSource.conventional)
If no path found by any method, returns (None, ConfigSource.builtin)
source field is never None

structured_side_effects.description: Checks existence of conventional config path in CWD (stat only, not read)
Reads EXEMPLAR_CONFIG environment variable via os.environ.get

functions.name: load_config
functions.description: Primary public API for configuration loading. Resolves config path via resolve_config_path, reads and parses the YAML file if one exists, deep-merges parsed content onto default_config() values, and validates the result via ExemplarConfig.model_validate(). Returns default_config() if no config file is found (builtin source). An empty YAML file (null document) also returns default_config(). All errors are wrapped in the ConfigError hierarchy with clear messages including the resolved path.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: Optional explicit config file path. Passed through to resolve_config_path.

error_cases.name: file_not_found
error_cases.condition: Resolved path (from explicit or env_var source) does not exist on filesystem

error_cases.name: yaml_parse_error
error_cases.condition: YAML file exists but contains invalid YAML syntax

error_data.message: Failed to parse YAML config at {resolved_path}: {yaml_error}

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: Parsed YAML content fails Pydantic model validation (type errors, constraint violations, cross-field invariant failures)

error_cases.name: permission_denied
error_cases.condition: Config file exists but is not readable due to filesystem permissions

postconditions: Returned ExemplarConfig passes all model validators and field constraints
If no config file exists and no explicit path given, result equals default_config()
All LedgerFieldRule patterns in returned config compile as valid regex
CircuitConfig cross-field invariants hold (parallel_stages ⊆ stages, threshold ordering)
An empty YAML document returns default_config()

structured_side_effects.description: Reads YAML configuration file from disk at the resolved path. File content is parsed via yaml.safe_load.
Delegates to resolve_config_path which may stat the conventional config path

functions.name: config_to_yaml
functions.description: Serializes an ExemplarConfig back to a YAML string. Useful for generating config templates, dumping effective configuration for debugging, and round-trip testing. Uses Pydantic model_dump() with mode='json' for serialization compatibility, then yaml.dump().
preconditions: config is a valid ExemplarConfig instance
postconditions: Returned string is valid YAML that can be parsed by yaml.safe_load
yaml.safe_load(result) fed back through ExemplarConfig.model_validate produces an equivalent config

invariants: All configuration models are frozen (immutable) after construction
default_config() always returns a valid ExemplarConfig that passes all validators
load_config() with no arguments and no config file present returns default_config()
Config path resolution order is always: explicit > env_var > conventional > builtin
parallel_stages is always a subset of stages in any valid CircuitConfig
block_threshold >= warn_threshold in any valid CircuitConfig
LedgerFieldRule.pattern is always a compilable regex
ReviewerConfig.scope_patterns contains only valid glob patterns
CircuitConfig.excluded_file_patterns contains only valid glob patterns
An empty YAML file (null document) produces default_config()
Every public function carries a PACT key annotation for Sentinel attribution
No Chronicler events are emitted from this module; callers handle event emission

data_access.rationale: Reads YAML configuration files from the local filesystem containing INTERNAL-tier data (stage ordering, reviewer settings, secret/PII detection regex patterns, file glob exclusions, Apprentice storage paths). These files may encode organizational security policy via LedgerFieldRule patterns. No PII or secrets are stored in config files themselves; the patterns describe how to detect them elsewhere. No writes: config is read-only at load time.

side_effects.rationale: Reads a YAML config file from disk at a resolved path. File contents are INTERNAL as they encode review policy, secret detection patterns, and infrastructure paths.
Reads the EXEMPLAR_CONFIG environment variable during path resolution to locate a user-specified configuration file path.

authority.rationale: The config component is the single authoritative source for all Exemplar configuration data: it owns the canonical shape of configuration models, the default values factory, the path resolution chain, and the validation rules. No other component may define or mutate configuration structures.
